questions.question: What is the 'stored-program concept' fundamental to the Von Neumann architecture? (2 marks)
expectedPoints: The concept that program instructions are stored in memory alongside data.
This allows the computer to be easily reprogrammed by changing the contents of memory, as opposed to being hard-wired for a specific task.

questions.question: Describe, with the aid of a diagram, the Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle. Explain the role of the PC, MAR, MDR, and IR registers in the Fetch phase. (10 marks)
expectedPoints: Draw a labeled diagram showing the cycle as a three-step process (Fetch, Decode, Execute) with arrows indicating repetition.
Fetch Phase: The PC holds the address of the next instruction. This address is copied to the MAR.
A 'Memory Read' signal is issued. The instruction at that address is retrieved and placed on the data bus.
This instruction is loaded into the CPU and stored in the IR.
The PC is incremented to prepare for the next instruction fetch.
Decode Phase: The Control Unit interprets the opcode in the IR to determine the operation.
Execute Phase: The CU generates control signals to perform the operation (e.g., activate the ALU, access registers, perform a memory write).
